description: Regression scenarios for the Codex gate/mechanical/judgment evaluation harness: inventory evidence, mechanical scorer determinism, judgment-tier merge, prioritized backlog, and explicit baseline updates.

evals.name: mechanical-only-mode
evals.prompt: toolkit skill들 구조만 빠르게 점수 매겨줘 (mechanical only)
expected_behavior: Runs inventory_gate.py for the stated scope unless the user explicitly asked for score.py only.
Reports deterministic skill axes S2/S3/S5/S6/S7 and leaves S1/S4/S8 judgment-deferred (·).
Explicitly states mechanical mode is partial and final_total is unavailable.
anti_behavior: Hand-computing structure scores instead of reading score.py output.
Presenting mechanical points as the complete grade.

evals.name: full-cycle-with-backlog
evals.prompt: toolkit 전체 평가하고 개선 우선순위 뽑아줘
expected_behavior: Runs inventory_gate.py, then the mechanical scorer, then judgment scoring with the skill or agent rubric.
Every judgment-axis score carries a file:line citation.
Emits a prioritized backlog for dimensions below 2, with gate failures ranked first.
Writes Codex-only report artifacts under docs/skill-agent-eval/codex/ when artifacts are requested.
anti_behavior: Scoring Claude Code paths as Codex assets.
Producing a scorecard with no prioritized backlog.
Overwriting the mechanical baseline without an explicit --update-baseline step.

evals.name: ci-regression-gate
expected_behavior: Runs score.py --ci, compares per-axis mechanical scores against history.mechanical.json.
Exits non-zero if any mechanical sub-score dropped vs the prior baseline.
Does not rewrite history.mechanical.json in CI mode.
Tells the operator to run --update-baseline if the baseline is missing or intentionally stale.
anti_behavior: Blocking on judgment-tier variance (judge scores must never gate CI).
Comparing mean totals instead of per-item per-axis sub-scores.
